<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
<meta charset="UTF-8">
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
<title>Rome Recycled — Material Afterlives in the Ancient City</title>
<link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Cormorant+Garamond:ital,wght@0,300;0,400;0,600;0,700;1,300;1,400&family=JetBrains+Mono:wght@300;400;500&family=DM+Sans:wght@300;400;500;600&display=swap" rel="stylesheet">
<style>
:root {
  --terracotta: #C4533A;
  --terracotta-light: #E8785F;
  --travertine: #F5EDE0;
  --parchment: #FAF6EE;
  --dark-earth: #2b251c;
  --bronze: #8B7355;
  --bronze-light: #A89070;
  --patina: #5B7A6B;
  --patina-light: #7A9D8B;
  --marble-white: #F0ECE4;
  --deep-shadow: #0D0B08;
  --gold: #C4A95A;
  --gold-dim: #9A8545;
  --slate: #3A3530;
  --ink: #2A2520;
}

* { margin: 0; padding: 0; box-sizing: border-box; }

html { scroll-behavior: smooth; }

body {
  font-family: 'DM Sans', sans-serif;
  background: var(--dark-earth);
  color: var(--travertine);
  overflow-x: hidden;
  -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased;
}

::selection {
  background: var(--terracotta);
  color: var(--parchment);
}

/* HERO */
.hero {
  min-height: 100vh;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  justify-content: flex-end;
  padding: 60px;
  position: relative;
  overflow: hidden;
}

.hero-bg {
  position: absolute;
  inset: 0;
  background: 
    radial-gradient(ellipse 80% 60% at 70% 30%, rgba(196,83,58,0.12) 0%, transparent 70%),
    radial-gradient(ellipse 50% 50% at 20% 80%, rgba(91,122,107,0.1) 0%, transparent 60%),
    linear-gradient(180deg, var(--deep-shadow) 0%, var(--dark-earth) 100%);
}

.hero-grid {
  position: absolute;
  inset: 0;
  opacity: 0.04;
  background-image: 
    linear-gradient(var(--bronze) 1px, transparent 1px),
    linear-gradient(90deg, var(--bronze) 1px, transparent 1px);
  background-size: 80px 80px;
}

.hero-content { position: relative; z-index: 2; }

.hero-label {
  font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace;
  font-size: 11px;
  letter-spacing: 4px;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  color: var(--terracotta-light);
  margin-bottom: 24px;
  opacity: 0;
  animation: fadeUp 1s 0.3s forwards;
}

.hero h1 {
  font-family: 'Cormorant Garamond', serif;
  font-weight: 300;
  font-size: clamp(48px, 8vw, 120px);
  line-height: 0.95;
  color: var(--travertine);
  margin-bottom: 32px;
  opacity: 0;
  animation: fadeUp 1s 0.5s forwards;
}

.hero h1 em {
  font-style: italic;
  color: var(--terracotta-light);
}

.hero-subtitle {
  font-family: 'Cormorant Garamond', serif;
  font-size: clamp(18px, 2.5vw, 28px);
  font-weight: 300;
  color: var(--bronze-light);
  max-width: 600px;
  line-height: 1.5;
  opacity: 0;
  animation: fadeUp 1s 0.7s forwards;
}

.hero-scroll {
  margin-top: 48px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 12px;
  font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace;
  font-size: 10px;
  letter-spacing: 3px;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  color: var(--bronze);
  opacity: 0;
  animation: fadeUp 1s 1s forwards;
}

.scroll-line {
  width: 40px;
  height: 1px;
  background: var(--bronze);
  animation: pulse 2s infinite;
}

@keyframes fadeUp {
  from { opacity: 0; transform: translateY(20px); }
  to { opacity: 1; transform: translateY(0); }
}

@keyframes pulse {
  0%, 100% { opacity: 0.3; }
  50% { opacity: 1; }
}

/* NAV */
.nav-bar {
  position: sticky;
  top: 0;
  z-index: 100;
  background: rgba(26,22,16,0.92);
  backdrop-filter: blur(20px);
  border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(139,115,85,0.15);
  padding: 0 60px;
  display: flex;
  gap: 0;
  overflow-x: auto;
}

.nav-bar button {
  background: none;
  border: none;
  font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace;
  font-size: 10px;
  letter-spacing: 2px;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  color: var(--bronze);
  padding: 18px 24px;
  cursor: pointer;
  white-space: nowrap;
  position: relative;
  transition: color 0.3s;
}

.nav-bar button:hover,
.nav-bar button.active {
  color: var(--terracotta-light);
}

.nav-bar button.active::after {
  content: '';
  position: absolute;
  bottom: 0;
  left: 24px;
  right: 24px;
  height: 2px;
  background: var(--terracotta);
}

/* SECTIONS */
.section {
  padding: 80px 60px;
  display: none;
}

.section.active { display: block; }

.section-header {
  margin-bottom: 60px;
}

.section-label {
  font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace;
  font-size: 10px;
  letter-spacing: 4px;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  color: var(--terracotta);
  margin-bottom: 16px;
}

.section-title {
  font-family: 'Cormorant Garamond', serif;
  font-weight: 300;
  font-size: clamp(32px, 5vw, 64px);
  line-height: 1.1;
  color: var(--travertine);
  margin-bottom: 20px;
}

.section-desc {
  font-size: 15px;
  color: var(--bronze-light);
  max-width: 640px;
  line-height: 1.7;
}

/* INTERACTIVE MAP */
.map-container {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: 1fr 380px;
  gap: 40px;
  margin-top: 40px;
}

.map-svg-wrap {
  background: rgba(26,22,16,0.6);
  border: 1px solid rgba(139,115,85,0.15);
  border-radius: 8px;
  padding: 40px;
  position: relative;
  min-height: 560px;
}

.map-svg-wrap svg {
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
}

.map-pin {
  cursor: pointer;
  transition: all 0.3s;
}

.map-pin:hover circle.pin-outer {
  r: 10;
  fill: var(--terracotta-light);
}

.map-pin circle.pin-outer {
  fill: var(--terracotta);
  transition: all 0.3s;
}

.map-pin circle.pin-pulse {
  fill: var(--terracotta);
  opacity: 0.3;
  animation: mapPulse 2s infinite;
}

@keyframes mapPulse {
  0% { r: 6; opacity: 0.4; }
  100% { r: 18; opacity: 0; }
}

.map-sidebar {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  gap: 12px;
  max-height: 560px;
  overflow-y: auto;
}

.map-sidebar::-webkit-scrollbar { width: 3px; }
.map-sidebar::-webkit-scrollbar-track { background: transparent; }
.map-sidebar::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb { background: var(--bronze); border-radius: 3px; }

.map-card {
  background: rgba(139,115,85,0.06);
  border: 1px solid rgba(139,115,85,0.12);
  border-radius: 6px;
  padding: 20px;
  cursor: pointer;
  transition: all 0.3s;
}

.map-card:hover, .map-card.active {
  background: rgba(196,83,58,0.08);
  border-color: var(--terracotta);
}

.map-card-name {
  font-family: 'Cormorant Garamond', serif;
  font-size: 18px;
  font-weight: 600;
  color: var(--travertine);
  margin-bottom: 4px;
}

.map-card-date {
  font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace;
  font-size: 10px;
  color: var(--terracotta-light);
  letter-spacing: 1px;
  margin-bottom: 8px;
}

.map-card-desc {
  font-size: 13px;
  color: var(--bronze-light);
  line-height: 1.6;
}

.map-card-materials {
  display: flex;
  flex-wrap: wrap;
  gap: 6px;
  margin-top: 10px;
}

.mat-tag {
  font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace;
  font-size: 9px;
  letter-spacing: 1px;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  padding: 4px 8px;
  border-radius: 3px;
  background: rgba(91,122,107,0.15);
  color: var(--patina-light);
  border: 1px solid rgba(91,122,107,0.2);
}

.mat-tag.marble { background: rgba(240,236,228,0.08); color: var(--marble-white); border-color: rgba(240,236,228,0.15); }
.mat-tag.stone { background: rgba(139,115,85,0.1); color: var(--bronze-light); border-color: rgba(139,115,85,0.2); }
.mat-tag.brick { background: rgba(196,83,58,0.1); color: var(--terracotta-light); border-color: rgba(196,83,58,0.2); }
.mat-tag.sculpture { background: rgba(196,169,90,0.1); color: var(--gold); border-color: rgba(196,169,90,0.2); }

/* BEYOND ROME */
.beyond-rome {
  margin-top: 60px;
  border-top: 1px solid rgba(139,115,85,0.12);
  padding-top: 48px;
}

.beyond-rome-header {
  display: flex;
  align-items: baseline;
  gap: 16px;
  margin-bottom: 24px;
}

.beyond-rome-title {
  font-family: 'Cormorant Garamond', serif;
  font-size: 28px;
  font-weight: 300;
  color: var(--travertine);
}

.beyond-rome-sub {
  font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace;
  font-size: 10px;
  letter-spacing: 2px;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  color: var(--bronze);
}

.beyond-grid {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(320px, 1fr));
  gap: 16px;
}

.beyond-card {
  background: rgba(91,122,107,0.04);
  border: 1px solid rgba(91,122,107,0.12);
  border-radius: 6px;
  padding: 20px;
  transition: all 0.3s;
}

.beyond-card:hover {
  border-color: var(--patina);
  background: rgba(91,122,107,0.08);
}

.beyond-card-place {
  font-family: 'Cormorant Garamond', serif;
  font-size: 19px;
  font-weight: 600;
  color: var(--patina-light);
  margin-bottom: 4px;
}

.beyond-card-region {
  font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace;
  font-size: 9px;
  letter-spacing: 2px;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  color: var(--bronze);
  margin-bottom: 10px;
}

.beyond-card-desc {
  font-size: 13px;
  color: var(--bronze-light);
  line-height: 1.6;
}

/* MATERIAL FLOWS */
.flows-grid {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(300px, 1fr));
  gap: 24px;
  margin-top: 40px;
}

.flow-card {
  background: rgba(26,22,16,0.6);
  border: 1px solid rgba(139,115,85,0.12);
  border-radius: 8px;
  padding: 32px;
  position: relative;
  overflow: hidden;
  transition: all 0.4s;
}

.flow-card:hover {
  border-color: rgba(196,83,58,0.3);
  transform: translateY(-2px);
}

.flow-card::before {
  content: '';
  position: absolute;
  top: 0;
  left: 0;
  right: 0;
  height: 3px;
}

.flow-card.granite::before { background: linear-gradient(90deg, #C4533A, #E8785F); }
.flow-card.pavonazzetto::before { background: linear-gradient(90deg, #F0ECE4, #A89070); }
.flow-card.proconnesian::before { background: linear-gradient(90deg, #7A9D8B, #5B7A6B); }
.flow-card.amphora::before { background: linear-gradient(90deg, #C4A95A, #8B7355); }

.flow-origin {
  font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace;
  font-size: 10px;
  letter-spacing: 3px;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  color: var(--terracotta-light);
  margin-bottom: 8px;
}

.flow-material {
  font-family: 'Cormorant Garamond', serif;
  font-size: 28px;
  font-weight: 600;
  color: var(--travertine);
  margin-bottom: 16px;
}

.flow-distance {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 12px;
  margin-bottom: 16px;
}

.flow-distance-line {
  flex: 1;
  height: 2px;
  background: linear-gradient(90deg, var(--terracotta), transparent);
  position: relative;
}

.flow-distance-line::after {
  content: '';
  position: absolute;
  right: 0;
  top: -3px;
  width: 8px;
  height: 8px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: var(--terracotta);
}

.flow-km {
  font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace;
  font-size: 22px;
  font-weight: 500;
  color: var(--gold);
}

.flow-km span {
  font-size: 10px;
  color: var(--bronze);
  letter-spacing: 1px;
}

.flow-detail {
  font-size: 13px;
  color: var(--bronze-light);
  line-height: 1.6;
}

/* LABOR SAVINGS VIZ */
.labor-viz {
  margin-top: 40px;
}

.labor-chart {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: 200px 1fr 80px;
  gap: 16px;
  align-items: center;
  margin-bottom: 20px;
}

.labor-label {
  font-family: 'Cormorant Garamond', serif;
  font-size: 16px;
  color: var(--travertine);
  text-align: right;
}

.labor-bar-track {
  height: 40px;
  background: rgba(139,115,85,0.08);
  border-radius: 4px;
  overflow: hidden;
  position: relative;
}

.labor-bar {
  height: 100%;
  border-radius: 4px;
  transition: width 1.5s cubic-bezier(0.25, 0.46, 0.45, 0.94);
  position: relative;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  padding-left: 16px;
}

.labor-bar.bar-original { background: linear-gradient(90deg, var(--terracotta), var(--terracotta-light)); }
.labor-bar.bar-half { background: linear-gradient(90deg, var(--patina), var(--patina-light)); }
.labor-bar.bar-tufelli { background: linear-gradient(90deg, var(--bronze), var(--bronze-light)); }
.labor-bar.bar-reticulate { background: linear-gradient(90deg, var(--gold-dim), var(--gold)); }

.labor-pct {
  font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace;
  font-size: 24px;
  font-weight: 500;
  color: var(--gold);
}

.labor-extra {
  margin-top: 48px;
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr);
  gap: 24px;
}

.labor-stat {
  background: rgba(139,115,85,0.06);
  border: 1px solid rgba(139,115,85,0.1);
  border-radius: 8px;
  padding: 28px;
  text-align: center;
}

.labor-stat-num {
  font-family: 'Cormorant Garamond', serif;
  font-size: 48px;
  font-weight: 300;
  color: var(--terracotta-light);
  line-height: 1;
  margin-bottom: 8px;
}

.labor-stat-unit {
  font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace;
  font-size: 10px;
  letter-spacing: 2px;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  color: var(--bronze);
  margin-bottom: 12px;
}

.labor-stat-desc {
  font-size: 13px;
  color: var(--bronze-light);
  line-height: 1.5;
}

/* MATERIALS CATALOGUE */
.materials-grid {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(280px, 1fr));
  gap: 20px;
  margin-top: 40px;
}

.material-item {
  border: 1px solid rgba(139,115,85,0.12);
  border-radius: 8px;
  cursor: pointer;
  transition: all 0.4s;
  position: relative;
  overflow: hidden;
  background: rgba(26,22,16,0.5);
}

.material-item:hover {
  border-color: var(--terracotta);
  transform: translateY(-3px);
  box-shadow: 0 8px 32px rgba(0,0,0,0.3);
}

.material-img-wrap {
  width: 100%;
  height: 160px;
  overflow: hidden;
  position: relative;
}

.material-img-wrap img {
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  object-fit: cover;
  filter: sepia(0.15) brightness(0.85);
  transition: all 0.5s;
}

.material-item:hover .material-img-wrap img {
  filter: sepia(0) brightness(1);
  transform: scale(1.05);
}

.material-img-wrap::after {
  content: '';
  position: absolute;
  bottom: 0;
  left: 0;
  right: 0;
  height: 60px;
  background: linear-gradient(transparent, rgba(26,22,16,0.8));
}

.material-body {
  padding: 20px;
}

.material-icon-inline {
  display: inline-block;
  width: 28px;
  height: 28px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  text-align: center;
  line-height: 28px;
  font-size: 14px;
  margin-right: 8px;
  vertical-align: middle;
}

.material-name {
  font-family: 'Cormorant Garamond', serif;
  font-size: 20px;
  font-weight: 600;
  color: var(--travertine);
  margin-bottom: 8px;
}

.material-uses {
  font-size: 12px;
  color: var(--bronze-light);
  line-height: 1.6;
}

.material-source {
  margin-top: 10px;
  font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace;
  font-size: 9px;
  letter-spacing: 1px;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  color: var(--terracotta);
  opacity: 0.7;
}

/* PHASE CHANGE */
.phase-intro {
  max-width: 720px;
  margin-bottom: 48px;
}

.phase-intro p {
  font-size: 16px;
  color: var(--bronze-light);
  line-height: 1.8;
  margin-bottom: 16px;
}

.phase-intro p em {
  color: var(--terracotta-light);
  font-style: italic;
}

/* ENTROPY INTERACTIVE CHART */
.entropy-viz {
  margin: 48px 0;
  background: rgba(26,22,16,0.6);
  border: 1px solid rgba(139,115,85,0.12);
  border-radius: 12px;
  overflow: hidden;
}

.entropy-chart-wrap {
  padding: 40px 40px 20px;
  position: relative;
}

.entropy-chart-title {
  font-family: 'Cormorant Garamond', serif;
  font-size: 24px;
  font-weight: 300;
  color: var(--travertine);
  margin-bottom: 8px;
}

.entropy-chart-sub {
  font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace;
  font-size: 10px;
  letter-spacing: 2px;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  color: var(--bronze);
  margin-bottom: 32px;
}

.entropy-svg-container {
  width: 100%;
  position: relative;
}

.entropy-svg-container svg {
  width: 100%;
  height: auto;
  display: block;
}

/* Phase selector strip */
.entropy-phases {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: repeat(6, 1fr);
  border-top: 1px solid rgba(139,115,85,0.1);
}

.entropy-phase-btn {
  background: none;
  border: none;
  border-right: 1px solid rgba(139,115,85,0.08);
  padding: 16px 12px;
  cursor: pointer;
  transition: all 0.4s;
  text-align: center;
  position: relative;
}

.entropy-phase-btn:last-child { border-right: none; }

.entropy-phase-btn::before {
  content: '';
  position: absolute;
  top: 0;
  left: 0;
  right: 0;
  height: 3px;
  background: var(--terracotta);
  transform: scaleX(0);
  transition: transform 0.4s;
}

.entropy-phase-btn:hover { background: rgba(196,83,58,0.04); }
.entropy-phase-btn.active { background: rgba(196,83,58,0.06); }
.entropy-phase-btn.active::before { transform: scaleX(1); }

.entropy-phase-num {
  font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace;
  font-size: 9px;
  letter-spacing: 2px;
  color: var(--bronze);
  margin-bottom: 4px;
  transition: color 0.3s;
}

.entropy-phase-btn.active .entropy-phase-num { color: var(--terracotta-light); }

.entropy-phase-label {
  font-family: 'Cormorant Garamond', serif;
  font-size: 15px;
  font-weight: 600;
  color: var(--bronze-light);
  transition: color 0.3s;
}

.entropy-phase-btn.active .entropy-phase-label { color: var(--travertine); }

/* Detail panel */
.entropy-detail {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: 200px 1fr;
  gap: 40px;
  padding: 40px;
  border-top: 1px solid rgba(139,115,85,0.1);
  min-height: 280px;
  transition: opacity 0.3s;
}

.entropy-detail-illust {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  gap: 16px;
}

.entropy-block-viz {
  width: 200px;
  height: 200px;
  position: relative;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
}

.entropy-detail-info { display: flex; flex-direction: column; justify-content: center; }

.entropy-detail-name {
  font-family: 'Cormorant Garamond', serif;
  font-size: 36px;
  font-weight: 300;
  color: var(--travertine);
  margin-bottom: 4px;
  line-height: 1.1;
}

.entropy-detail-phase-type {
  font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace;
  font-size: 11px;
  letter-spacing: 3px;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  color: var(--terracotta-light);
  margin-bottom: 16px;
}

.entropy-detail-stats {
  display: flex;
  gap: 32px;
  margin-bottom: 20px;
}

.entropy-stat-item {}

.entropy-stat-value {
  font-family: 'Cormorant Garamond', serif;
  font-size: 32px;
  font-weight: 300;
  color: var(--gold);
  line-height: 1;
}

.entropy-stat-label {
  font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace;
  font-size: 9px;
  letter-spacing: 2px;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  color: var(--bronze);
  margin-top: 4px;
}

.entropy-detail-desc {
  font-size: 14px;
  color: var(--bronze-light);
  line-height: 1.7;
  max-width: 560px;
}

.entropy-detail-example {
  margin-top: 12px;
  font-size: 13px;
  color: var(--patina-light);
  line-height: 1.6;
  font-style: italic;
}

@media (max-width: 900px) {
  .entropy-phases { grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr); }
  .entropy-detail { grid-template-columns: 1fr; }
  .entropy-detail-illust { order: -1; }
}

.mosaic-example {
  margin-top: 48px;
  background: rgba(139,115,85,0.06);
  border: 1px solid rgba(139,115,85,0.1);
  border-radius: 8px;
  padding: 40px;
}

.mosaic-title {
  font-family: 'Cormorant Garamond', serif;
  font-size: 28px;
  font-weight: 300;
  color: var(--travertine);
  margin-bottom: 20px;
}

.mosaic-grid {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: repeat(12, 1fr);
  gap: 3px;
  margin: 24px 0;
  max-width: 500px;
}

.mosaic-tile {
  aspect-ratio: 1;
  border-radius: 2px;
  transition: all 0.3s;
  cursor: pointer;
}

.mosaic-tile:hover {
  transform: scale(1.2);
  z-index: 2;
}

.mosaic-desc {
  font-size: 14px;
  color: var(--bronze-light);
  line-height: 1.7;
  max-width: 600px;
  margin-top: 20px;
}

/* TIMELINE */
.timeline-wrap {
  margin-top: 40px;
  position: relative;
  padding-left: 40px;
}

.timeline-line {
  position: absolute;
  left: 16px;
  top: 0;
  bottom: 0;
  width: 2px;
  background: linear-gradient(180deg, var(--terracotta), var(--patina), var(--bronze));
}

.timeline-item {
  position: relative;
  margin-bottom: 40px;
  padding-left: 32px;
}

.timeline-dot {
  position: absolute;
  left: -32px;
  top: 6px;
  width: 12px;
  height: 12px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: var(--terracotta);
  border: 2px solid var(--dark-earth);
}

.timeline-date {
  font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace;
  font-size: 11px;
  letter-spacing: 2px;
  color: var(--terracotta-light);
  margin-bottom: 6px;
}

.timeline-title {
  font-family: 'Cormorant Garamond', serif;
  font-size: 22px;
  font-weight: 600;
  color: var(--travertine);
  margin-bottom: 8px;
}

.timeline-text {
  font-size: 14px;
  color: var(--bronze-light);
  line-height: 1.7;
  max-width: 600px;
}

/* LEGAL BOX */
.legal-box {
  margin-top: 48px;
  background: rgba(196,83,58,0.05);
  border: 1px solid rgba(196,83,58,0.2);
  border-radius: 8px;
  padding: 32px;
  max-width: 700px;
}

.legal-box h3 {
  font-family: 'Cormorant Garamond', serif;
  font-size: 24px;
  color: var(--terracotta-light);
  margin-bottom: 16px;
}

.legal-box p {
  font-size: 14px;
  color: var(--bronze-light);
  line-height: 1.7;
}

/* FOOTER */
.site-footer {
  padding: 60px;
  border-top: 1px solid rgba(139,115,85,0.1);
  display: flex;
  justify-content: space-between;
  align-items: flex-end;
}

.footer-left {
  font-family: 'Cormorant Garamond', serif;
  font-size: 14px;
  color: var(--bronze);
}

.footer-right {
  font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace;
  font-size: 10px;
  color: var(--bronze);
  letter-spacing: 2px;
}

/* POPUP */
.popup-overlay {
  display: none;
  position: fixed;
  inset: 0;
  background: rgba(13,11,8,0.85);
  z-index: 200;
  justify-content: center;
  align-items: center;
  padding: 40px;
}

.popup-overlay.show { display: flex; }

.popup {
  background: var(--dark-earth);
  border: 1px solid rgba(139,115,85,0.2);
  border-radius: 12px;
  max-width: 600px;
  width: 100%;
  padding: 40px;
  position: relative;
  max-height: 80vh;
  overflow-y: auto;
}

.popup-close {
  position: absolute;
  top: 16px;
  right: 16px;
  background: none;
  border: none;
  color: var(--bronze);
  font-size: 24px;
  cursor: pointer;
}

.popup h2 {
  font-family: 'Cormorant Garamond', serif;
  font-size: 32px;
  color: var(--travertine);
  margin-bottom: 8px;
}

.popup .popup-sub {
  font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace;
  font-size: 10px;
  color: var(--terracotta-light);
  letter-spacing: 2px;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  margin-bottom: 20px;
}

.popup p {
  font-size: 14px;
  color: var(--bronze-light);
  line-height: 1.7;
  margin-bottom: 12px;
}

/* RESPONSIVE */
@media (max-width: 900px) {
  .hero, .section, .site-footer { padding: 32px; }
  .nav-bar { padding: 0 16px; }
  .map-container { grid-template-columns: 1fr; }
  .labor-chart { grid-template-columns: 120px 1fr 60px; }
  .labor-extra { grid-template-columns: 1fr; }
  .site-footer { flex-direction: column; gap: 16px; }
}
</style>
</head>
<body>

<!-- HERO -->
<section class="hero">
  <div class="hero-bg"></div>
  <div class="hero-grid"></div>
  <div class="hero-content">
    <div class="hero-label">After Simon J. Barker &middot; Volume 63</div>
    <h1>Rome<br><em>Recycled</em></h1>
    <p class="hero-subtitle">Material afterlives in the ancient city &mdash; an interactive exploration of how Roman builders deconstructed, salvaged, and recycled their built world.</p>
    <div class="hero-scroll">
      <span class="scroll-line"></span>
      <span>Scroll to explore</span>
    </div>
  </div>
</section>

<!-- NAV -->
<nav class="nav-bar" id="nav">
  <button class="active" data-section="map">Map of Reuse</button>
  <button data-section="flows">Material Origins</button>
  <button data-section="labor">Labor Saved</button>
  <button data-section="materials">Materials Catalogue</button>
  <button data-section="phase">Phase-Change</button>
  <button data-section="timeline">Legal &amp; Social</button>
</nav>

<!-- MAP SECTION -->
<div class="section active" id="sec-map">
  <div class="section-header">
    <div class="section-label">01 &mdash; Interactive Map</div>
    <h2 class="section-title">Where Recycling Happened</h2>
    <p class="section-desc">Key Roman sites where material salvage and reuse shaped the built environment. Each pin marks a building or location documented in Barker's study. Click to explore.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="map-container">
    <div class="map-svg-wrap">
      <svg viewBox="0 0 700 520" id="mapSvg">
        <!-- Stylized Rome map background -->
        <defs>
          <linearGradient id="riverGrad" x1="0" y1="0" x2="1" y2="0">
            <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#5B7A6B" stop-opacity="0.15"/>
            <stop offset="50%" stop-color="#5B7A6B" stop-opacity="0.25"/>
            <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#5B7A6B" stop-opacity="0.1"/>
          </linearGradient>
        </defs>
        <!-- Tiber River -->
        <path d="M 180 0 Q 200 80 170 160 Q 140 240 180 300 Q 210 360 190 420 Q 170 480 200 520" stroke="url(#riverGrad)" stroke-width="28" fill="none" stroke-linecap="round"/>
        <path d="M 180 0 Q 200 80 170 160 Q 140 240 180 300 Q 210 360 190 420 Q 170 480 200 520" stroke="#5B7A6B" stroke-width="1" fill="none" stroke-opacity="0.3" stroke-dasharray="4 4"/>
        
        <!-- Hills labels -->
        <text x="340" y="145" font-family="JetBrains Mono" font-size="8" fill="#8B7355" opacity="0.5" letter-spacing="3">PALATINE</text>
        <text x="420" y="100" font-family="JetBrains Mono" font-size="8" fill="#8B7355" opacity="0.5" letter-spacing="3">OPPIAN</text>
        <text x="500" y="260" font-family="JetBrains Mono" font-size="8" fill="#8B7355" opacity="0.5" letter-spacing="3">CAELIAN</text>
        <text x="450" y="340" font-family="JetBrains Mono" font-size="8" fill="#8B7355" opacity="0.5" letter-spacing="3">ESQUILINE</text>
        <text x="230" y="400" font-family="JetBrains Mono" font-size="8" fill="#8B7355" opacity="0.5" letter-spacing="3">AVENTINE</text>
        <text x="90" y="480" font-family="JetBrains Mono" font-size="7" fill="#8B7355" opacity="0.4" letter-spacing="2">MONTE TESTACCIO</text>
        
        <!-- City wall suggestion -->
        <ellipse cx="350" cy="260" rx="280" ry="220" fill="none" stroke="#8B7355" stroke-width="0.5" stroke-opacity="0.15" stroke-dasharray="8 8"/>
        <text x="580" y="180" font-family="JetBrains Mono" font-size="7" fill="#8B7355" opacity="0.3" letter-spacing="2" transform="rotate(30 580 180)">AURELIAN WALL</text>
        
        <!-- Map pins will be added by JS -->
      </svg>
    </div>
    <div class="map-sidebar" id="mapSidebar"></div>
  </div>

  <div class="beyond-rome">
    <div class="beyond-rome-header">
      <div class="beyond-rome-title">Beyond Rome</div>
      <div class="beyond-rome-sub">Recycling across the Empire</div>
    </div>
    <p class="section-desc" style="margin-bottom:24px;">Roman material reuse extended far beyond the capital. These sites, documented in Barker's essay and the Fant/Russell/Barker Pompeii study, reveal how recycling practices operated across different contexts, climates, and centuries.</p>
    <div class="beyond-grid" id="beyondGrid"></div>
  </div>
</div>

<!-- MATERIAL FLOWS -->
<div class="section" id="sec-flows">
  <div class="section-header">
    <div class="section-label">02 &mdash; Material Origins</div>
    <h2 class="section-title">Across the Mediterranean</h2>
    <p class="section-desc">Roman builders sourced stone from quarries across the empire. The immense labor and distance involved in transporting these materials made salvage and reuse an economic imperative.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="flows-grid" id="flowsGrid"></div>
</div>

<!-- LABOR SAVINGS -->
<div class="section" id="sec-labor">
  <div class="section-header">
    <div class="section-label">03 &mdash; Economics of Reuse</div>
    <h2 class="section-title">Labor Saved Through Recycling</h2>
    <p class="section-desc">Based on 19th-century labor rate comparisons, reusing tufa ashlar blocks yielded dramatic cost savings depending on the form of reprocessing.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="labor-viz" id="laborViz">
    <div class="labor-chart">
      <div class="labor-label">Original function</div>
      <div class="labor-bar-track"><div class="labor-bar bar-original" style="width:0%" data-target="77"></div></div>
      <div class="labor-pct" data-val="77">0%</div>
    </div>
    <div class="labor-chart">
      <div class="labor-label">Half-sized ashlars</div>
      <div class="labor-bar-track"><div class="labor-bar bar-half" style="width:0%" data-target="63"></div></div>
      <div class="labor-pct" data-val="63">0%</div>
    </div>
    <div class="labor-chart">
      <div class="labor-label">Tufelli blocks</div>
      <div class="labor-bar-track"><div class="labor-bar bar-tufelli" style="width:0%" data-target="27"></div></div>
      <div class="labor-pct" data-val="27">0%</div>
    </div>
    <div class="labor-chart">
      <div class="labor-label">Reticulate cubes</div>
      <div class="labor-bar-track"><div class="labor-bar bar-reticulate" style="width:0%" data-target="27"></div></div>
      <div class="labor-pct" data-val="27">0%</div>
    </div>

    <div class="labor-extra">
      <div class="labor-stat">
        <div class="labor-stat-num">3,285</div>
        <div class="labor-stat-unit">Days of quarrying</div>
        <div class="labor-stat-desc">Required for 184 pavonazzetto columns in the Forum of Trajan</div>
      </div>
      <div class="labor-stat">
        <div class="labor-stat-num">9,200</div>
        <div class="labor-stat-unit">Days of carving</div>
        <div class="labor-stat-desc">Additional labor to finish those same 184 column shafts</div>
      </div>
      <div class="labor-stat">
        <div class="labor-stat-num">350</div>
        <div class="labor-stat-unit">Tons per cargo</div>
        <div class="labor-stat-desc">Maximum stone weight found in shipwrecks bound for Rome</div>
      </div>
    </div>

    <div style="margin-top:48px;">
      <p class="section-desc">Standard demolition was estimated to be 25&ndash;50% faster than careful demolition. Yet the savings from careful salvage &mdash; preserving intact blocks for higher-value reuse &mdash; meant that meticulous deconstruction was economically justified. A whole ashlar block could be progressively reduced: first whole, then half-sized, then into tufelli, then reticulate cubes, and finally rubble. At each stage, the stone retained functional and economic value.</p>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>

<!-- MATERIALS CATALOGUE -->
<div class="section" id="sec-materials">
  <div class="section-header">
    <div class="section-label">04 &mdash; Materials Catalogue</div>
    <h2 class="section-title">What Was Recycled</h2>
    <p class="section-desc">The range of materials salvaged from Roman buildings. Everything from monumental marble columns to fragments of broken ceramics found a second life. Data drawn from Barker's study of Rome and the Fant, Russell &amp; Barker survey of 8,135 marble fragments across 57 bar counters at Pompeii and Herculaneum.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="materials-grid" id="materialsGrid"></div>
</div>

<!-- PHASE CHANGE -->
<div class="section" id="sec-phase">
  <div class="section-header">
    <div class="section-label">05 &mdash; Phase-Change</div>
    <h2 class="section-title">Entropy &amp; Retained Value</h2>
  </div>
  <div class="phase-intro">
    <p>In your framework of Rome as a <em>hinged informational surface</em>, phase-change describes how materials undergo transformations &mdash; shifting state while retaining embedded labor, meaning, and structural potential. Unlike modern waste which loses all value, Roman materials cascaded through declining states of order while maintaining utility at every stage.</p>
    <p>Two examples anchor this concept: the <em>mosaic tesserae</em>, whose standardized geometry allowed infinite reconfiguration; and the <em>ashlar block</em>, whose progressive reduction from whole block to rubble traces a clear entropy gradient &mdash; yet at every phase, the stone held value.</p>
  </div>

  <div class="entropy-viz" id="entropyViz">
    <div class="entropy-chart-wrap">
      <div class="entropy-chart-title">The Entropy Ladder</div>
      <div class="entropy-chart-sub">Tufa Ashlar Blocks &mdash; Labor Savings vs. Entropy</div>
      <div class="entropy-svg-container">
        <svg viewBox="0 0 800 300" id="entropySvg"></svg>
      </div>
    </div>
    <div class="entropy-phases" id="entropyPhases"></div>
    <div class="entropy-detail" id="entropyDetail"></div>
  </div>

  <div class="mosaic-example">
    <div class="mosaic-title">Sectilia Pavimenta &mdash; Infinite Reconfiguration</div>
    <p class="mosaic-desc" style="margin-bottom:20px;">Decorative floors made of geometric stone pieces based on the Roman foot. The standardized sizes and simple shapes (squares, triangles) meant tiles could be stripped from one floor and recombined into entirely different patterns. Click tiles to see them rearrange &mdash; same material, new information.</p>
    <div class="mosaic-grid" id="mosaicGrid"></div>
    <button onclick="shuffleMosaic()" style="margin-top:16px;background:rgba(196,83,58,0.15);border:1px solid var(--terracotta);color:var(--terracotta-light);padding:10px 20px;border-radius:4px;cursor:pointer;font-family:'JetBrains Mono',monospace;font-size:11px;letter-spacing:2px;">RECONFIGURE TILES</button>
  </div>
</div>

<!-- LEGAL & SOCIAL TIMELINE -->
<div class="section" id="sec-timeline">
  <div class="section-header">
    <div class="section-label">06 &mdash; Legal &amp; Social Context</div>
    <h2 class="section-title">Regulation, Craft, &amp; Ideology</h2>
    <p class="section-desc">Recycling in Rome was not anarchic &mdash; it was carefully legislated, professionally managed, and at times ideologically charged.</p>
  </div>

  <div class="legal-box">
    <h3>Roman Demolition Law</h3>
    <p>Reuse was a legitimate and carefully legislated endeavor. Roman jurists produced numerous laws from the late Republican period through the sixth century AD. The main goal was to prevent the purchase of buildings solely for profitable demolition. Civic authorities worried about uncontrolled destruction undermining individual buildings and the aesthetic coherence of cities.</p>
    <p style="margin-top:12px;">There was a key distinction: private demolition was always regulated, while civic or imperial authorities had broader scope to demolish and decide how materials were reused.</p>
  </div>

  <div class="timeline-wrap" id="timelineWrap" style="margin-top:48px;"></div>
</div>

<!-- POPUP -->
<div class="popup-overlay" id="popupOverlay">
  <div class="popup" id="popupContent">
    <button class="popup-close" onclick="closePopup()">&times;</button>
  </div>
</div>

<!-- FOOTER -->
<footer class="site-footer">
  <div class="footer-left">
    Based on &ldquo;Rome Recycled&rdquo; by Simon J. Barker &middot; Volume 63, Archis 2023<br>
    &amp; &ldquo;Marble Use and Reuse at Pompeii and Herculaneum&rdquo; by Fant, Russell &amp; Barker (PBSR 2013)<br>
    <em>The Not-So-Easy Guide to Circular Interior Design</em>
  </div>
  <div class="footer-right">Interactive study companion</div>
</footer>

<script>
// DATA
const mapLocations = [
  { id:'arch-constantine', name:'Arch of Constantine', x:380, y:220, date:'AD 315', materials:['marble'],
    desc:'Built from ~16,000 marble blocks entirely salvaged from earlier monuments. Even the newly carved Constantinian reliefs used recycled blocks. A watershed moment for architectural reuse.' },
  { id:'domus-aurea', name:'Domus Aurea', x:420, y:150, date:'AD 64–109', materials:['marble','stone'],
    desc:'Nero\'s Golden House covered ~100 hectares. When Trajan built his Baths over the Oppian Pavilion, ~5,000 m² of marble wall and floor decoration was systematically stripped from 65 of 140 rooms.' },
  { id:'forum-trajan', name:'Forum of Trajan', x:330, y:175, date:'AD 106–112', materials:['marble'],
    desc:'Its 184 pavonazzetto marble columns required 3,285 days of quarrying and 9,200 days of carving — making salvage of such material immensely valuable.' },
  { id:'basilica-julia', name:'Basilica Julia', x:320, y:210, date:'54 BC – AD 12', materials:['stone','brick'],
    desc:'During Augustan reconstruction, Sempronian foundation blocks were removed, recut into reticulate blocks for wall facing, and broken into rubble for concrete core — one source, three new uses.' },
  { id:'house-augustus', name:'House of Augustus / Temple of Apollo', x:345, y:240, date:'42–28 BC', materials:['marble','brick'],
    desc:'The house was demolished for the Temple of Apollo. Marble sectilia pavimenta were completely stripped, leaving only mortar imprints. Rooms were filled with sorted demolition debris.' },
  { id:'vigna-barberini', name:'Vigna Barberini Terrace', x:370, y:250, date:'AD 80s–190s', materials:['marble','brick','stone'],
    desc:'Two phases of systematic salvage. After the first, bricks were separated by type. After a fire ca. AD 191, lead pipes, marble, bricks, and even rubble core were recovered.' },
  { id:'aurelian-wall', name:'Aurelian Wall', x:500, y:290, date:'AD 271–275', materials:['sculpture','stone'],
    desc:'Before construction of the 19 km wall circuit, abandoned 1st–3rd century statues on the Esquiline and Caelian hills were smashed and used as foundation material for new houses.' },
  { id:'santa-sabina', name:'Santa Sabina', x:240, y:370, date:'5th century AD', materials:['marble'],
    desc:'24 fluted Proconnesian marble columns with matching 2nd-century Corinthian capitals were reused — likely sourced from a warehouse. The name Rufenus carved on one column identifies the middleman.' },
  { id:'san-lorenzo', name:'San Lorenzo fuori le Mura', x:520, y:160, date:'6th century AD', materials:['sculpture','marble'],
    desc:'Two capitals carved with classical trophaea (war spoils) were deliberately reused near the tomb of St. Lawrence — repurposing pagan victory symbols for Christian triumphalism.' },
  { id:'porticus-octavia', name:'Porticus of Octavia', x:280, y:260, date:'Severan period', materials:['marble'],
    desc:'During Severan alterations, marble column drums were reworked and standardized so that signs of their original function were invisible — recycling made invisible.' },
  { id:'colosseum', name:'Colosseum', x:410, y:205, date:'AD 508+', materials:['stone','marble'],
    desc:'Under Theodoric, the northern half was restored for use while the non-functional southern half became a sanctioned "disassembly yard." Senator Gerontius held the salvage concession.' },
  { id:'monte-testaccio', name:'Monte Testaccio', x:160, y:430, date:'Ongoing', materials:['ceramic'],
    desc:'A 35m-high hill of 20,000 m² made of millions of broken Spanish olive oil amphorae. These broke into large curved pieces unsuitable for construction reuse — the exception that proves the rule.' },
];

const materialFlows = [
  { origin:'Aswan & Shellal, Egypt', material:'Pink Granite (Granito Rosso)', km:'4,000+', kmNum:4000,
    detail:'Transported 1,000 km along the Nile to Alexandria, then 3,000+ km by sea to Rome. Used for columns and veneer from the later 1st century AD. Many Egyptian granite columns survive recycled in medieval Roman churches.',
    cls:'granite' },
  { origin:'Dokimeion, Turkey', material:'Pavonazzetto Marble', km:'2,500+', kmNum:2500,
    detail:'White marble with distinctive purple veining. 184 column shafts in the Forum of Trajan required 12,485 days of combined quarrying and carving labor.',
    cls:'pavonazzetto' },
  { origin:'Proconnesos (Marmara)', material:'Proconnesian Marble', km:'1,500+', kmNum:1500,
    detail:'Widely exported fluted columns and Corinthian capitals. 24 matching columns reused in the 5th-century church of Santa Sabina, possibly from a secondhand warehouse.',
    cls:'proconnesian' },
  { origin:'Baetica, Spain', material:'Olive Oil Amphorae', km:'2,000+', kmNum:2000,
    detail:'Most amphorae could be washed and reused, or broken for aggregate. Spanish variants broke into large curved pieces unsuitable for reuse — hence Monte Testaccio, the 35m amphora mountain.',
    cls:'amphora' },
  { origin:'North Africa & Greece', material:'Various Marbles & Stones', km:'1,000–3,000', kmNum:2000,
    detail:'Shipwrecks bound for Rome carried stone cargoes up to 350 tons. The long-distance movement of colossal quantities made every piece worth salvaging.',
    cls:'granite' },
];

const matImgData = {
  "Marble": "data:image/jpeg;base64,",
  "Stone": "data:image/jpeg;base64,",
  "Brick": "data:image/jpeg;base64,",
  "Roof Tiles": "data:image/jpeg;base64,",
  "Wood": "data:image/jpeg;base64,",
  "Glass": "data:image/jpeg;base64,",
  "Lead": "data:image/jpeg;base64,",
  "Wall Paintings": "data:image/jpeg;base64,",
  "Mosaic Tesserae": "data:image/jpeg;base64,",
  "Sculpture": "data:image/jpeg;base64,",
  "Concrete Rubble": "data:image/jpeg;base64,",
  "Amphorae": "data:image/jpeg;base64,"
};

const materialsData = [
  { name:'Marble', icon:'\u25C7', color:'#F0ECE4', source:'Caupona of Asellina, Pompeii (Fant et al. 2013)',
    uses:'The most sought-after and frequently reused material. Used for columns, capitals, floor tiles (sectilia pavimenta), wall revetment, and sculpture. At Pompeii, 46% of bars had marble-clad counters. Over 8,000 marble pieces were documented across 57 bars. Luna marble was the default white marble, with Pentelic also attested.',
    detail:'Marble was stripped so completely from buildings like the Domus Aurea that not a single piece remained. At Pompeii and Herculaneum, bar counters used fragments of marble salvaged from elite houses and public buildings\u2014about 75% of panels were broken rather than cut on all sides.' },
  { name:'Stone', icon:'\u25A3', color:'#A89070', source:'Opus reticulatum wall, Pompeii',
    uses:'Ashlar blocks (tufa, travertine, peperino) were progressively reduced across multiple reuse cycles. First whole, then half-sized, then tufelli, then reticulate cubes, then rubble\u2014retaining economic value at every stage.',
    detail:'Reusing tufa ashlar blocks saved 77% of labor costs if used for their original function, 63% if reprocessed into half-sized ashlars, and 27% if reprocessed into tufelli or reticulate blocks. Blocks from the Basilica Sempronia were recut into three different new forms during the Augustan reconstruction of the Basilica Julia.' },
  { name:'Brick', icon:'\u25A4', color:'#C4533A', source:'Bar IV.15 at Herculaneum (Barker, drawing)',
    uses:'Produced in standardized sizes based on the Roman foot. Salvaged whole for their original purpose or broken for rubble in concrete. Vitruvius mentions bricks made from old roof-tiles (veteres tegulae).',
    detail:'At the Vigna Barberini on the Palatine, bricks were carefully separated by type during deconstruction. Second-hand brick was specifically valued for its strength\u2014recycled materials were used to help undertake the salvage of higher quality materials.' },
  { name:'Roof Tiles', icon:'\u2302', color:'#E8785F', source:'Bar at Herculaneum (Barker)',
    uses:'Reused as bricks or broken for rubble in concrete construction. A painted sign in Pompeii Insula III.7 advertises the sale of salvaged roof tiles: imbrices and gutter-tiles from demolished houses.',
    detail:'The Pompeii advertisement (CIL IV 7124) is direct evidence for a commercial market in secondhand building materials. It dates to the late Republican period, showing that organized salvage trade predates the later empire.' },
  { name:'Wood', icon:'\u2AE0', color:'#8B7355', source:'Roman ruins, architectural remains',
    uses:'Generally poorly preserved in the archaeological record. A rare exception at Herculaneum: excavators found a carpenter\u2019s workshop stacked with timbers felled over a period of two centuries.',
    detail:'Many timbers showed signs of previous use and reuse\u2014nail holes or multiple cuttings (trenches for half-lap joins). The Vesuvian preservation gives unique insight into what was likely a widespread but archaeologically invisible practice.' },
  { name:'Glass', icon:'\u25C8', color:'#7A9D8B', source:'Bar V.9-10, Herculaneum\u2014polychrome materials',
    uses:'Salvaged from windows and vessels. Could be remelted for new glass objects or crushed and reused as aggregate material in construction.',
    detail:'Glass fragments joined the wider stream of recyclable materials in Roman cities. Like marble, glass retained value through multiple transformations\u2014from vessel to crushed aggregate to concrete fill.' },
  { name:'Lead', icon:'\u25C9', color:'#6A6560', source:'Roman concrete and infrastructure',
    uses:'Pipes, clamps, dowels, and waterproofing. Systematically recovered during demolition\u2014even during the Vigna Barberini salvage operations, lead pipes were specifically targeted for recovery.',
    detail:'Lead\'s high recycling value meant it was among the first materials stripped during deconstruction. At the Vigna Barberini, the systematic recovery included lead pipes, marble wall revetment, bricks, mortar, and even rubble from the concrete core.' },
  { name:'Wall Paintings', icon:'\u25A8', color:'#C4A95A', source:'Opus sectile face, bar at V.4.6-8, Pompeii',
    uses:'Rarely reused for their decorative purpose (a notable case: the Casa dei Quattro Stili in Pompeii). Usually recycled as fill for foundations or as aggregate in mortared rubble construction.',
    detail:'At Ostia and Aquileia, plaster fragments were specifically reused in floor preparation layers to combat moisture problems. At Aquileia and Cremona, rising water levels required repeated floor-raising with thick layers of draining material including recycled painted plaster.' },
  { name:'Mosaic Tesserae', icon:'\u25A6', color:'#5B7A6B', source:'Opus sectile decorative floor patterns',
    uses:'Stripped from floors for reconfiguration into new patterns. Evidence at Pompeii (Room 15, Casa delle Vestali), Ostia, and extensively in Roman Britain (Colchester, Littlecote Park).',
    detail:'The standardized geometry of tesserae made them infinitely recyclable. At Pompeii, 170 reused opus sectile panels were recorded on bar counters; at Herculaneum, 125. Standard shapes (rectangles, triangles, discs, hexagons, diamonds) could be recombined freely.' },
  { name:'Sculpture', icon:'\u2656', color:'#9A8545', source:'Arch of Constantine, Rome (16th c. engraving)',
    uses:'Re-carved into new likenesses (damnatio memoriae of \u201Cbad\u201D emperors), statue bases recycled with new inscriptions, or smashed for building material in foundations.',
    detail:'From the 3rd century onward, virtually all statue bases were secondhand. An estimated 50% of surviving Late Antique public statuary was reused. The Arch of Constantine incorporated ~16,000 marble blocks from earlier monuments\u2014the ultimate act of sculptural recycling.' },
  { name:'Concrete Rubble', icon:'\u229E', color:'#3A3530', source:'Roman concrete vault construction',
    uses:'Opus caementicium absorbed all \u201Cwaste\u201D material. Its strength came from pozzolana mortar, not the aggregate\u2014any debris (stone, brick, tile, pottery, plaster) could serve as caementa.',
    detail:'Concrete construction was invented in the mid-2nd century BC and was inherently a recycling technology from inception. It provided a quick, economic, and strong technique that disposed of demolition debris while creating durable new structures.' },
  { name:'Amphorae', icon:'\u23DA', color:'#A89070', source:'Cipollino and bardiglio panels, Pompeii bars',
    uses:'Ceramic transport containers. Most could be washed and reused, or broken for aggregate. The exception: Spanish olive oil amphorae, which formed Monte Testaccio\u2014a 35m-high hill of 20,000 m\u00B2.',
    detail:'Monte Testaccio consists of 85-95% Spanish amphorae whose curved fragments were unsuitable for construction reuse. Thin-walled African amphorae, by contrast, broke into flat pieces ideal for concrete aggregate\u2014demonstrating how material properties determined recyclability.' },
];

const timelineData = [
  { date:'Late Republic–6th c. AD', title:'Demolition Law Tradition', text:'Roman jurists produced continuous legislation governing demolition. The core prohibition: buying buildings solely for profitable demolition. Private demolition was always regulated; civic authorities had more latitude.' },
  { date:'4th century AD', title:'The Oxyrhynchos Papyrus (P.Oxy I.71)', text:'A papyrus from Egypt lists columns in buildings: their number, location, condition (standing, overturned, broken), and whether made from local or imported stone. A template for systematic salvage planning.' },
  { date:'AD 508', title:'Theodoric & the Colosseum', text:'Ostrogothic king Theodoric ordered the northern Colosseum restored for use while allowing the non-functional southern half to be salvaged. Senator Gerontius may have held the disassembly concession.' },
  { date:'ca. AD 507–511', title:'Cassiodorus & Heritage Maintenance', text:'In his Variae, Cassiodorus records Theodoric ordering columns and marble from derelict Aestuna sent to Ravenna — framing reuse as heritage restoration, not mere scavenging.' },
  { date:'5th century AD', title:'Rufenus at Santa Sabina', text:'An inscribed name on a reused column identifies a middleman — the official or merchant who sourced 24 matching Proconnesian marble columns, likely from a warehouse of secondhand material.' },
  { date:'3rd century onwards', title:'Statue Base Recycling', text:'Virtually all new statue bases were composed of secondhand bases with new texts. An estimated 50% of surviving Late Antique public statuary was reused. Prefect Probianus relocated fallen statues to the Roman Forum.' },
  { date:'Late Antiquity', title:'Religious Appropriation', text:'Classical trophaea (war spoils) on capitals at San Lorenzo fuori le Mura were reinterpreted as symbols of Christ\'s victory — deliberate ideological reuse alongside practical salvage.' },
];

const beyondRomeData = [
  { place:'Pompeii', region:'Campania, Italy',
    desc:'Ubiquitous marble reuse on bar counters: 49 bars documented with 6,047 marble fragments. Casa dei Quattro Stili: rare decorative reuse of a wall-painting maenad. Casa delle Vestali Room 15: opus sectile completely stripped in the Augustan period. Temple of Fortuna: wall-painting fragments recycled as podium aggregate. Post-AD 62 earthquake, salvaged materials flooded the secondhand market. A painted sign in Insula III.7 advertises the sale of salvaged roof tiles.' },
  { place:'Herculaneum', region:'Campania, Italy',
    desc:'Eight marble-clad bar counters with 2,088 fragments \u2014 far more polychrome than Pompeii. A carpenter\u2019s workshop preserved by Vesuvius contained timbers felled over 200 years, many showing nail holes and multiple cuttings from previous use. Bar V.9\u201310 had 42 reused opus sectile panels, miniature cipollino pilasters, and eight rare Egyptian granite fragments.' },
  { place:'Ostia', region:'Port of Rome, Lazio',
    desc:'The port city of Rome. Baths of Neptune: Hadrianic rebuilding reused capitals and bases from the earlier Domitianic phase (AD 81\u201396). House IV.II.6 contained a large number of secondhand tesserae awaiting recycling. Seven marble-clad bar counters survived, assigned by Meiggs to the 3rd century AD when the secondhand marble market peaked. Plaster fragments reused in floor preparation layers to combat humidity.' },
  { place:'Roman Britain', region:'Britannia',
    desc:'Mosaic tesserae recycling was especially common here. At Middleborough in Colchester, a late-3rd-century floor shows evidence of tesserae scraped from the concrete base. At Littlecote Park in Wiltshire, recycled tesserae are identifiable in the later mosaic by traces of mortar from their previous installation.' },
  { place:'Aquileia & Cremona', region:'Northeastern Italy',
    desc:'Rising water levels forced repeated construction phases to raise floor levels with thick layers of draining material \u2014 including recycled painted plaster. Here, the frequent recycling of painted plaster was both economical (readily available from demolition) and practical (effective drainage).' },
  { place:'Athens', region:'Greece',
    desc:'A temple built during the Classical period was systematically dismantled, moved wholesale, and rebuilt near the middle of the Athenian Agora during the Augustan Age. To ensure blocks were re-set correctly, a detailed system of marking them with letters was used \u2014 an extraordinary example of planned deconstruction and reassembly.' },
  { place:'Oxyrhynchos', region:'Egypt (modern al-Bahnasa)',
    desc:'A 4th-century AD papyrus (P.Oxy I.71) contains a systematic list of columns from buildings in an unnamed city: their number, location within the building, condition (standing, overturned, or broken), and whether made from local or imported stone, with marble specifically noted. A template for ancient salvage planning.' },
  { place:'Ravenna', region:'Northern Italy',
    desc:'In his Variae (3.9, ca. AD 507\u2013511), Cassiodorus records Theodoric ordering columns and marble from the derelict municipium of Aestuna sent to Ravenna \u2014 framing reuse as heritage restoration rather than scavenging: \u201Cthose stones ought to rise up, renovated for embellishment.\u201D' },
];

// RENDER BEYOND ROME
function renderBeyondRome() {
  const grid = document.getElementById('beyondGrid');
  beyondRomeData.forEach(b => {
    grid.innerHTML += `
      <div class="beyond-card">
        <div class="beyond-card-place">${b.place}</div>
        <div class="beyond-card-region">${b.region}</div>
        <div class="beyond-card-desc">${b.desc}</div>
      </div>
    `;
  });
}

// RENDER MAP
function renderMap() {
  const svg = document.getElementById('mapSvg');
  const sidebar = document.getElementById('mapSidebar');
  
  mapLocations.forEach((loc, i) => {
    // SVG pin
    const g = document.createElementNS('http://www.w3.org/2000/svg', 'g');
    g.classList.add('map-pin');
    g.setAttribute('data-id', loc.id);
    g.innerHTML = `
      <circle class="pin-pulse" cx="${loc.x}" cy="${loc.y}" r="6"/>
      <circle class="pin-outer" cx="${loc.x}" cy="${loc.y}" r="6"/>
      <circle cx="${loc.x}" cy="${loc.y}" r="2.5" fill="#FAF6EE"/>
    `;
    g.addEventListener('click', () => highlightLocation(loc.id));
    g.addEventListener('mouseenter', () => highlightLocation(loc.id));
    svg.appendChild(g);

    // Sidebar card
    const card = document.createElement('div');
    card.className = 'map-card';
    card.setAttribute('data-id', loc.id);
    card.innerHTML = `
      <div class="map-card-name">${loc.name}</div>
      <div class="map-card-date">${loc.date}</div>
      <div class="map-card-desc">${loc.desc}</div>
      <div class="map-card-materials">
        ${loc.materials.map(m => `<span class="mat-tag ${m}">${m}</span>`).join('')}
      </div>
    `;
    card.addEventListener('click', () => highlightLocation(loc.id));
    sidebar.appendChild(card);
  });
}

function highlightLocation(id) {
  document.querySelectorAll('.map-card').forEach(c => c.classList.remove('active'));
  document.querySelectorAll('.map-pin').forEach(p => {
    p.querySelectorAll('.pin-outer').forEach(o => { o.style.fill = ''; o.setAttribute('r', '6'); });
  });
  
  const card = document.querySelector(`.map-card[data-id="${id}"]`);
  const pin = document.querySelector(`.map-pin[data-id="${id}"]`);
  if (card) { card.classList.add('active'); card.scrollIntoView({ behavior:'smooth', block:'nearest' }); }
  if (pin) { 
    pin.querySelector('.pin-outer').style.fill = '#E8785F'; 
    pin.querySelector('.pin-outer').setAttribute('r', '10');
  }
}

// RENDER FLOWS
function renderFlows() {
  const grid = document.getElementById('flowsGrid');
  materialFlows.forEach(f => {
    grid.innerHTML += `
      <div class="flow-card ${f.cls}">
        <div class="flow-origin">${f.origin}</div>
        <div class="flow-material">${f.material}</div>
        <div class="flow-distance">
          <div class="flow-distance-line"></div>
          <div class="flow-km">${f.km} <span>KM</span></div>
        </div>
        <div class="flow-detail">${f.detail}</div>
      </div>
    `;
  });
}

// ANIMATE LABOR BARS
function animateLabor() {
  document.querySelectorAll('.labor-bar').forEach(bar => {
    const target = bar.dataset.target;
    setTimeout(() => { bar.style.width = target + '%'; }, 300);
  });
  document.querySelectorAll('.labor-pct').forEach(el => {
    const target = parseInt(el.dataset.val);
    let current = 0;
    const interval = setInterval(() => {
      current += 1;
      if (current > target) { clearInterval(interval); current = target; }
      el.textContent = current + '%';
    }, 20);
  });
}

// RENDER MATERIALS
function renderMaterials() {
  const grid = document.getElementById('materialsGrid');
  materialsData.forEach(m => {
    const imgSrc = matImgData[m.name] || '';
    grid.innerHTML += `
      <div class="material-item" onclick="showMaterialPopup('${m.name}')">
        ${imgSrc ? `<div class="material-img-wrap"><img src="${imgSrc}" alt="${m.name}" loading="lazy"/></div>` : ''}
        <div class="material-body">
          <div class="material-name"><span class="material-icon-inline" style="background:${m.color}20;color:${m.color};border:1px solid ${m.color}40;">${m.icon}</span>${m.name}</div>
          <div class="material-uses">${m.uses.substring(0, 90)}…</div>
          <div class="material-source">${m.source || ''}</div>
        </div>
      </div>
    `;
  });
}

function showMaterialPopup(name) {
  const m = materialsData.find(x => x.name === name);
  if (!m) return;
  const imgSrc = matImgData[name] || '';
  const overlay = document.getElementById('popupOverlay');
  const content = document.getElementById('popupContent');
  content.innerHTML = `
    <button class="popup-close" onclick="closePopup()">&times;</button>
    ${imgSrc ? `<img src="${imgSrc}" alt="${name}" style="width:100%;height:200px;object-fit:cover;border-radius:8px;margin-bottom:20px;"/>` : ''}
    <h2>${m.name}</h2>
    <div class="popup-sub">${m.source || 'Material Profile'}</div>
    <p>${m.uses}</p>
    ${m.detail ? `<p style="margin-top:12px;border-top:1px solid rgba(139,115,85,0.15);padding-top:12px;">${m.detail}</p>` : ''}
  `;
  overlay.classList.add('show');
}

function closePopup() {
  document.getElementById('popupOverlay').classList.remove('show');
}

document.getElementById('popupOverlay').addEventListener('click', (e) => {
  if (e.target === e.currentTarget) closePopup();
});

// ENTROPY INTERACTIVE CHART
const entropyPhases = [
  { id: 0, name: 'Whole Ashlar', phaseType: 'Solid', labor: 77, entropy: 5,
    desc: 'Quarried, transported, and carefully shaped. The block retains its full geological integrity. Maximum structural value \u2014 used for load-bearing walls, foundations, and monumental facades.',
    example: 'The Basilica Sempronia\u2019s large tufa blocks were the starting point before being recut during the Augustan reconstruction of the Basilica Julia.',
    svg: '<rect x="30" y="30" width="100" height="100" rx="4" fill="#A89070" opacity="0.85"/><rect x="35" y="35" width="90" height="90" rx="2" fill="none" stroke="#F5EDE0" stroke-width="1" opacity="0.4"/><text x="80" y="85" text-anchor="middle" font-family="JetBrains Mono" font-size="8" fill="#F5EDE0" opacity="0.6">PRISTINE BLOCK</text>' },
  { id: 1, name: 'Half-Sized Ashlar', phaseType: 'Solid', labor: 63, entropy: 18,
    desc: 'The original block is split or recut into two pieces. Each retains structural capability. The cut reveals a new face but the stone\u2019s internal coherence is preserved.',
    example: 'At the Basilica Julia, spoliated Sempronian blocks were recut into half-sized ashlars for Augustan wall facing \u2014 one of three simultaneous reuse streams.',
    svg: '<rect x="20" y="40" width="55" height="80" rx="3" fill="#A89070" opacity="0.85"/><rect x="85" y="40" width="55" height="80" rx="3" fill="#A89070" opacity="0.7"/><line x1="78" y1="40" x2="78" y2="120" stroke="#C4533A" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="80" y="140" text-anchor="middle" font-family="JetBrains Mono" font-size="7" fill="#C4533A" opacity="0.7">SPLIT / RECUT</text>' },
  { id: 2, name: 'Tufelli', phaseType: 'Modular', labor: 27, entropy: 40,
    desc: 'Smaller rectangular blocks for wall facing. The stone transitions from unique structural element to standardized modular unit. Individual identity dissolves into pattern.',
    example: 'Tufa blocks from demolished Archaic houses on the Palatine Hill were reprocessed into tufelli for later concrete wall facing \u2014 the earliest examples of this recycling chain.',
    svg: '<rect x="15" y="35" width="30" height="22" rx="2" fill="#A89070" opacity="0.85"/><rect x="50" y="35" width="30" height="22" rx="2" fill="#A89070" opacity="0.78"/><rect x="85" y="35" width="30" height="22" rx="2" fill="#A89070" opacity="0.7"/><rect x="120" y="35" width="30" height="22" rx="2" fill="#A89070" opacity="0.65"/><rect x="15" y="62" width="30" height="22" rx="2" fill="#A89070" opacity="0.75"/><rect x="50" y="62" width="30" height="22" rx="2" fill="#A89070" opacity="0.7"/><rect x="85" y="62" width="30" height="22" rx="2" fill="#A89070" opacity="0.65"/><rect x="120" y="62" width="30" height="22" rx="2" fill="#A89070" opacity="0.6"/><rect x="15" y="89" width="30" height="22" rx="2" fill="#A89070" opacity="0.68"/><rect x="50" y="89" width="30" height="22" rx="2" fill="#A89070" opacity="0.6"/><rect x="85" y="89" width="30" height="22" rx="2" fill="#A89070" opacity="0.55"/><rect x="120" y="89" width="30" height="22" rx="2" fill="#A89070" opacity="0.5"/>' },
  { id: 3, name: 'Reticulate Cubes', phaseType: 'Surface / 2D', labor: 27, entropy: 55,
    desc: 'Pyramidal blocks with square faces, arranged diagonally in opus reticulatum. The stone becomes a decorative surface pattern \u2014 its depth compressed into a 2D geometric matrix around a concrete core.',
    example: 'The Basilica Julia\u2019s Sempronian blocks were also recut into reticulate cubes for Augustan wall facing \u2014 the same source material serving three entirely different architectural roles.',
    svg: '<g transform="rotate(45 80 80)"><rect x="45" y="45" width="18" height="18" fill="#A89070" opacity="0.75" stroke="#1A1610" stroke-width="0.5"/><rect x="65" y="45" width="18" height="18" fill="#A89070" opacity="0.7" stroke="#1A1610" stroke-width="0.5"/><rect x="85" y="45" width="18" height="18" fill="#A89070" opacity="0.65" stroke="#1A1610" stroke-width="0.5"/><rect x="45" y="65" width="18" height="18" fill="#A89070" opacity="0.7" stroke="#1A1610" stroke-width="0.5"/><rect x="65" y="65" width="18" height="18" fill="#A89070" opacity="0.65" stroke="#1A1610" stroke-width="0.5"/><rect x="85" y="65" width="18" height="18" fill="#A89070" opacity="0.6" stroke="#1A1610" stroke-width="0.5"/><rect x="45" y="85" width="18" height="18" fill="#A89070" opacity="0.65" stroke="#1A1610" stroke-width="0.5"/><rect x="65" y="85" width="18" height="18" fill="#A89070" opacity="0.6" stroke="#1A1610" stroke-width="0.5"/><rect x="85" y="85" width="18" height="18" fill="#A89070" opacity="0.55" stroke="#1A1610" stroke-width="0.5"/></g>' },
  { id: 4, name: 'Rubble / Caementa', phaseType: 'Liquid / Aggregate', labor: 10, entropy: 82,
    desc: 'Broken into irregular fragments and absorbed into opus caementicium (Roman concrete). The stone loses all geometric identity. Its strength now comes from the pozzolana mortar binding, not the aggregate itself.',
    example: 'Demolished masonry from the Vigna Barberini \u2014 fragments of stone, brick, tile, painted decoration, marble antefixes, mosaics \u2014 was all used as backfill aggregate. Only pieces too small to remove were left.',
    svg: '<circle cx="35" cy="55" r="9" fill="#A89070" opacity="0.6"/><circle cx="65" cy="45" r="7" fill="#A89070" opacity="0.55"/><circle cx="90" cy="65" r="11" fill="#A89070" opacity="0.5"/><circle cx="50" cy="80" r="6" fill="#A89070" opacity="0.45"/><circle cx="115" cy="50" r="8" fill="#A89070" opacity="0.5"/><circle cx="80" cy="95" r="5" fill="#A89070" opacity="0.4"/><circle cx="120" cy="85" r="10" fill="#A89070" opacity="0.45"/><circle cx="40" cy="100" r="7" fill="#A89070" opacity="0.4"/><circle cx="100" cy="105" r="6" fill="#A89070" opacity="0.35"/><circle cx="60" cy="110" r="4" fill="#A89070" opacity="0.35"/><circle cx="135" cy="70" r="5" fill="#A89070" opacity="0.4"/><circle cx="25" cy="80" r="4" fill="#A89070" opacity="0.35"/>' },
  { id: 5, name: 'Backfill', phaseType: 'Terrain / Ground', labor: 0, entropy: 100,
    desc: 'Dust, soil, and the smallest debris become part of the terrain itself. The material returns to the ground plane \u2014 no longer architecture, but earth. Zero labor savings: the material has completed its entropy journey.',
    example: 'At the Vigna Barberini, after all valuable materials were stripped, the remaining demolition debris was used as backfill to raise the terrace level. The architecture dissolved back into landscape.',
    svg: '<line x1="10" y1="90" x2="150" y2="90" stroke="#8B7355" stroke-width="1" opacity="0.3"/><circle cx="25" cy="88" r="2" fill="#A89070" opacity="0.3"/><circle cx="45" cy="89" r="1.5" fill="#A89070" opacity="0.25"/><circle cx="60" cy="87" r="1" fill="#A89070" opacity="0.2"/><circle cx="80" cy="89" r="2.5" fill="#A89070" opacity="0.25"/><circle cx="100" cy="88" r="1" fill="#A89070" opacity="0.2"/><circle cx="115" cy="89" r="1.5" fill="#A89070" opacity="0.2"/><circle cx="130" cy="88" r="1" fill="#A89070" opacity="0.15"/><line x1="10" y1="95" x2="150" y2="95" stroke="#8B7355" stroke-width="0.5" opacity="0.15" stroke-dasharray="3 5"/><text x="80" y="115" text-anchor="middle" font-family="JetBrains Mono" font-size="7" fill="#8B7355" opacity="0.4">TERRAIN</text>' },
];

let activePhase = 0;

function buildEntropyChart() {
  const svg = document.getElementById('entropySvg');
  const W = 800, H = 300;
  const pad = { top: 30, right: 40, bottom: 50, left: 60 };
  const cw = W - pad.left - pad.right;
  const ch = H - pad.top - pad.bottom;

  const xs = entropyPhases.map((p, i) => pad.left + (i / (entropyPhases.length - 1)) * cw);
  const laborYs = entropyPhases.map(p => pad.top + (1 - p.labor / 100) * ch);
  const entropyYs = entropyPhases.map(p => pad.top + (1 - p.entropy / 100) * ch);

  let svgContent = `
    <defs>
      <linearGradient id="laborFill" x1="0" y1="0" x2="0" y2="1">
        <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#C4A95A" stop-opacity="0.15"/>
        <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#C4A95A" stop-opacity="0"/>
      </linearGradient>
      <linearGradient id="entropyFill" x1="0" y1="0" x2="0" y2="1">
        <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#C4533A" stop-opacity="0.1"/>
        <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#C4533A" stop-opacity="0"/>
      </linearGradient>
      <filter id="glow"><feGaussianBlur stdDeviation="3" result="blur"/><feMerge><feMergeNode in="blur"/><feMergeNode in="SourceGraphic"/></feMerge></filter>
    </defs>`;

  // Grid lines
  for (let pct = 0; pct <= 100; pct += 25) {
    const y = pad.top + (1 - pct / 100) * ch;
    svgContent += `<line x1="${pad.left}" y1="${y}" x2="${W - pad.right}" y2="${y}" stroke="#8B7355" stroke-width="0.5" opacity="0.12"/>`;
    svgContent += `<text x="${pad.left - 10}" y="${y + 4}" text-anchor="end" font-family="JetBrains Mono" font-size="9" fill="#8B7355" opacity="0.5">${pct}%</text>`;
  }

  // Axes labels
  svgContent += `<text x="${pad.left - 10}" y="${pad.top - 12}" text-anchor="end" font-family="JetBrains Mono" font-size="8" fill="#8B7355" opacity="0.5" letter-spacing="1">%</text>`;

  // Labor savings area fill
  let laborArea = `M ${xs[0]} ${laborYs[0]}`;
  for (let i = 1; i < xs.length; i++) {
    const cpx = (xs[i - 1] + xs[i]) / 2;
    laborArea += ` C ${cpx} ${laborYs[i - 1]}, ${cpx} ${laborYs[i]}, ${xs[i]} ${laborYs[i]}`;
  }
  laborArea += ` L ${xs[xs.length - 1]} ${pad.top + ch} L ${xs[0]} ${pad.top + ch} Z`;
  svgContent += `<path d="${laborArea}" fill="url(#laborFill)"/>`;

  // Entropy area fill
  let entArea = `M ${xs[0]} ${entropyYs[0]}`;
  for (let i = 1; i < xs.length; i++) {
    const cpx = (xs[i - 1] + xs[i]) / 2;
    entArea += ` C ${cpx} ${entropyYs[i - 1]}, ${cpx} ${entropyYs[i]}, ${xs[i]} ${entropyYs[i]}`;
  }
  entArea += ` L ${xs[xs.length - 1]} ${pad.top + ch} L ${xs[0]} ${pad.top + ch} Z`;
  svgContent += `<path d="${entArea}" fill="url(#entropyFill)"/>`;

  // Labor savings line
  let laborPath = `M ${xs[0]} ${laborYs[0]}`;
  for (let i = 1; i < xs.length; i++) {
    const cpx = (xs[i - 1] + xs[i]) / 2;
    laborPath += ` C ${cpx} ${laborYs[i - 1]}, ${cpx} ${laborYs[i]}, ${xs[i]} ${laborYs[i]}`;
  }
  svgContent += `<path d="${laborPath}" fill="none" stroke="#C4A95A" stroke-width="2.5" id="laborLine" stroke-dasharray="1200" stroke-dashoffset="1200"><animate attributeName="stroke-dashoffset" from="1200" to="0" dur="1.5s" fill="freeze" begin="0.3s"/></path>`;

  // Entropy curve (dashed)
  let entPath = `M ${xs[0]} ${entropyYs[0]}`;
  for (let i = 1; i < xs.length; i++) {
    const cpx = (xs[i - 1] + xs[i]) / 2;
    entPath += ` C ${cpx} ${entropyYs[i - 1]}, ${cpx} ${entropyYs[i]}, ${xs[i]} ${entropyYs[i]}`;
  }
  svgContent += `<path d="${entPath}" fill="none" stroke="#C4533A" stroke-width="2" stroke-dasharray="6 4" opacity="0.7" id="entropyLine"><animate attributeName="stroke-dashoffset" from="1200" to="0" dur="2s" fill="freeze" begin="0.5s"/></path>`;

  // Legend
  svgContent += `<line x1="${W - 200}" y1="15" x2="${W - 175}" y2="15" stroke="#C4A95A" stroke-width="2.5"/>`;
  svgContent += `<text x="${W - 170}" y="19" font-family="JetBrains Mono" font-size="9" fill="#C4A95A" opacity="0.8">Labor Savings</text>`;
  svgContent += `<line x1="${W - 200}" y1="30" x2="${W - 175}" y2="30" stroke="#C4533A" stroke-width="2" stroke-dasharray="6 4"/>`;
  svgContent += `<text x="${W - 170}" y="34" font-family="JetBrains Mono" font-size="9" fill="#C4533A" opacity="0.8">Entropy</text>`;

  // Data points (clickable)
  entropyPhases.forEach((p, i) => {
    // Vertical guide line
    svgContent += `<line x1="${xs[i]}" y1="${Math.min(laborYs[i], entropyYs[i])}" x2="${xs[i]}" y2="${pad.top + ch}" stroke="#8B7355" stroke-width="0.5" opacity="0.08"/>`;
    // Phase label at bottom
    svgContent += `<text x="${xs[i]}" y="${H - 8}" text-anchor="middle" font-family="JetBrains Mono" font-size="8" fill="#8B7355" opacity="0.5" letter-spacing="1">${p.name.split(' ')[0].toUpperCase()}</text>`;

    // Labor dot
    svgContent += `<circle cx="${xs[i]}" cy="${laborYs[i]}" r="0" fill="#C4A95A" id="laborDot${i}" class="entropy-dot" data-phase="${i}" style="cursor:pointer"><animate attributeName="r" from="0" to="5" dur="0.3s" fill="freeze" begin="${0.3 + i * 0.15}s"/></circle>`;
    // Entropy dot
    svgContent += `<circle cx="${xs[i]}" cy="${entropyYs[i]}" r="0" fill="#C4533A" id="entDot${i}" class="entropy-dot" data-phase="${i}" style="cursor:pointer"><animate attributeName="r" from="0" to="4" dur="0.3s" fill="freeze" begin="${0.5 + i * 0.15}s"/></circle>`;

    // Active indicator (hidden initially)
    svgContent += `<circle cx="${xs[i]}" cy="${laborYs[i]}" r="12" fill="none" stroke="#C4A95A" stroke-width="1.5" opacity="0" id="activeRing${i}" filter="url(#glow)"/>`;
    // Hitbox
    svgContent += `<rect x="${xs[i] - 30}" y="${pad.top}" width="60" height="${ch}" fill="transparent" style="cursor:pointer" data-phase="${i}" class="entropy-hitbox"/>`;
  });

  svg.innerHTML = svgContent;

  // Add click handlers
  svg.querySelectorAll('.entropy-hitbox, .entropy-dot').forEach(el => {
    el.addEventListener('click', () => setEntropyPhase(parseInt(el.dataset.phase)));
  });
}

function buildEntropyPhaseButtons() {
  const container = document.getElementById('entropyPhases');
  entropyPhases.forEach((p, i) => {
    container.innerHTML += `
      <button class="entropy-phase-btn ${i === 0 ? 'active' : ''}" data-phase="${i}" onclick="setEntropyPhase(${i})">
        <div class="entropy-phase-num">0${i + 1}</div>
        <div class="entropy-phase-label">${p.name}</div>
      </button>
    `;
  });
}

function setEntropyPhase(idx) {
  activePhase = idx;
  const p = entropyPhases[idx];

  // Update buttons
  document.querySelectorAll('.entropy-phase-btn').forEach((btn, i) => {
    btn.classList.toggle('active', i === idx);
  });

  // Update chart active rings
  entropyPhases.forEach((_, i) => {
    const ring = document.getElementById('activeRing' + i);
    if (ring) {
      ring.setAttribute('opacity', i === idx ? '0.6' : '0');
      ring.setAttribute('r', i === idx ? '14' : '12');
    }
    const lDot = document.getElementById('laborDot' + i);
    if (lDot) lDot.setAttribute('r', i === idx ? '7' : '5');
  });

  // Update detail panel with animation
  const detail = document.getElementById('entropyDetail');
  detail.style.opacity = '0';
  setTimeout(() => {
    detail.innerHTML = `
      <div class="entropy-detail-illust">
        <div class="entropy-block-viz">
          <svg viewBox="0 0 200 200" width="200" height="200" id="phaseBlockSvg">${generatePhaseSvg(idx)}</svg>
        </div>
      </div>
      <div class="entropy-detail-info">
        <div class="entropy-detail-name">${p.name}</div>
        <div class="entropy-detail-phase-type">${p.phaseType}</div>
        <div class="entropy-detail-stats">
          <div class="entropy-stat-item">
            <div class="entropy-stat-value">${p.labor}%</div>
            <div class="entropy-stat-label">Labor Saved</div>
          </div>
          <div class="entropy-stat-item">
            <div class="entropy-stat-value" style="color:var(--terracotta-light)">${p.entropy}%</div>
            <div class="entropy-stat-label">Entropy</div>
          </div>
        </div>
        <div class="entropy-detail-desc">${p.desc}</div>
        <div class="entropy-detail-example">${p.example}</div>
      </div>
    `;
    detail.style.opacity = '1';
  }, 200);
}


function generatePhaseSvg(idx) {
  const d = 0.08; // base delay
  switch(idx) {
    case 0: // Whole Ashlar - block materializes with scale + glow
      return `
        <defs><filter id="blkGlow"><feGaussianBlur stdDeviation="4" result="b"/><feMerge><feMergeNode in="b"/><feMergeNode in="SourceGraphic"/></feMerge></filter></defs>
        <g filter="url(#blkGlow)">
          <rect x="50" y="50" width="100" height="100" rx="5" fill="#A89070" opacity="0">
            <animate attributeName="opacity" from="0" to="0.9" dur="0.6s" fill="freeze"/>
            <animate attributeName="rx" from="50" to="5" dur="0.8s" fill="freeze"/>
          </rect>
        </g>
        <rect x="55" y="55" width="90" height="90" rx="3" fill="none" stroke="#F5EDE0" stroke-width="1.2" opacity="0" stroke-dasharray="360" stroke-dashoffset="360">
          <animate attributeName="opacity" from="0" to="0.4" dur="0.3s" fill="freeze" begin="0.4s"/>
          <animate attributeName="stroke-dashoffset" from="360" to="0" dur="1s" fill="freeze" begin="0.4s"/>
        </rect>
        <text x="100" y="105" text-anchor="middle" font-family="JetBrains Mono" font-size="9" fill="#F5EDE0" opacity="0">
          PRISTINE<animate attributeName="opacity" from="0" to="0.5" dur="0.4s" fill="freeze" begin="0.8s"/>
        </text>
      `;

    case 1: // Half-Sized - one block splits into two
      return `
        <rect x="50" y="40" width="100" height="110" rx="4" fill="#A89070" opacity="0.85">
          <animate attributeName="width" from="100" to="45" dur="0.5s" fill="freeze" begin="0.3s"/>
          <animate attributeName="opacity" from="0.85" to="0.9" dur="0.5s" fill="freeze" begin="0.3s"/>
        </rect>
        <rect x="105" y="40" width="0" height="110" rx="4" fill="#A89070" opacity="0">
          <animate attributeName="width" from="0" to="45" dur="0.4s" fill="freeze" begin="0.5s"/>
          <animate attributeName="opacity" from="0" to="0.75" dur="0.4s" fill="freeze" begin="0.5s"/>
        </rect>
        <line x1="100" y1="35" x2="100" y2="155" stroke="#C4533A" stroke-width="2" opacity="0" stroke-dasharray="120" stroke-dashoffset="120">
          <animate attributeName="opacity" from="0" to="0.8" dur="0.2s" fill="freeze" begin="0.2s"/>
          <animate attributeName="stroke-dashoffset" from="120" to="0" dur="0.3s" fill="freeze" begin="0.2s"/>
        </line>
        <text x="100" y="175" text-anchor="middle" font-family="JetBrains Mono" font-size="8" fill="#C4533A" opacity="0">
          SPLIT<animate attributeName="opacity" from="0" to="0.6" dur="0.3s" fill="freeze" begin="0.7s"/>
        </text>
      `;

    case 2: // Tufelli - small blocks cascade in from above
      let tuf = '<defs><filter id="tufSh"><feDropShadow dx="0" dy="1" stdDeviation="1" flood-color="#000" flood-opacity="0.2"/></filter></defs>';
      const cols = 4, rows = 4, bw = 34, bh = 24, gap = 4, ox = 18, oy = 30;
      for (let r = 0; r < rows; r++) {
        for (let c = 0; c < cols; c++) {
          const x = ox + c * (bw + gap);
          const y = oy + r * (bh + gap);
          const delay = (0.1 + r * 0.12 + c * 0.06).toFixed(2);
          const op = (0.9 - r * 0.08 - c * 0.04).toFixed(2);
          tuf += `<rect x="${x}" y="${y}" width="${bw}" height="${bh}" rx="2" fill="#A89070" opacity="0" filter="url(#tufSh)">
            <animate attributeName="opacity" from="0" to="${op}" dur="0.3s" fill="freeze" begin="${delay}s"/>
            <animateTransform attributeName="transform" type="translate" from="0 -30" to="0 0" dur="0.4s" fill="freeze" begin="${delay}s"/>
          </rect>`;
        }
      }
      return tuf;

    case 3: // Reticulate cubes - diamond grid rotates into place
      let ret = '';
      const rSize = 22, rGap = 2, rOx = 48, rOy = 48;
      ret += `<g transform="translate(100,100)">`;
      for (let r = 0; r < 3; r++) {
        for (let c = 0; c < 3; c++) {
          const x = (c - 1) * (rSize + rGap);
          const y = (r - 1) * (rSize + rGap);
          const delay = (0.15 + r * 0.1 + c * 0.08).toFixed(2);
          const op = (0.8 - r * 0.06 - c * 0.04).toFixed(2);
          ret += `<rect x="${x - rSize/2}" y="${y - rSize/2}" width="${rSize}" height="${rSize}" fill="#A89070" opacity="0" stroke="#1A1610" stroke-width="0.5">
            <animate attributeName="opacity" from="0" to="${op}" dur="0.3s" fill="freeze" begin="${delay}s"/>
          </rect>`;
        }
      }
      ret += `<animateTransform attributeName="transform" type="rotate" from="0" to="45" dur="0.7s" fill="freeze" begin="0.1s" additive="sum"/>`;
      ret += `</g>`;
      return ret;

    case 4: // Rubble - pieces burst outward from center
      let rub = '';
      const pieces = [
        {cx:100,cy:100,r:12,tx:0,ty:0}, {cx:100,cy:100,r:9,tx:-35,ty:-30},
        {cx:100,cy:100,r:11,tx:40,ty:-25}, {cx:100,cy:100,r:7,tx:-45,ty:20},
        {cx:100,cy:100,r:10,tx:35,ty:35}, {cx:100,cy:100,r:8,tx:-15,ty:45},
        {cx:100,cy:100,r:6,tx:50,ty:5}, {cx:100,cy:100,r:9,tx:-40,ty:-5},
        {cx:100,cy:100,r:5,tx:10,ty:-50}, {cx:100,cy:100,r:7,tx:25,ty:50},
        {cx:100,cy:100,r:4,tx:-55,ty:35}, {cx:100,cy:100,r:6,tx:55,ty:-40},
      ];
      pieces.forEach((p, i) => {
        const delay = (0.05 + i * 0.04).toFixed(2);
        const op = (0.7 - i * 0.03).toFixed(2);
        rub += `<circle cx="${p.cx}" cy="${p.cy}" r="${p.r}" fill="#A89070" opacity="0">
          <animate attributeName="opacity" from="0" to="${op}" dur="0.3s" fill="freeze" begin="${delay}s"/>
          <animateTransform attributeName="transform" type="translate" from="0 0" to="${p.tx} ${p.ty}" dur="0.6s" fill="freeze" begin="${delay}s" calcMode="spline" keySplines="0.25 0.46 0.45 0.94"/>
        </circle>`;
      });
      return rub;

    case 5: // Backfill - particles rain down and settle at ground line
      let bf = `<line x1="20" y1="155" x2="180" y2="155" stroke="#8B7355" stroke-width="1" opacity="0">
        <animate attributeName="opacity" from="0" to="0.3" dur="0.5s" fill="freeze"/>
      </line>`;
      const particles = [
        {x:35,sz:3},{x:50,sz:2},{x:65,sz:2.5},{x:80,sz:3},{x:95,sz:1.5},
        {x:110,sz:2},{x:125,sz:2.5},{x:140,sz:1.5},{x:155,sz:2},{x:45,sz:1.5},
        {x:75,sz:2},{x:105,sz:1},{x:135,sz:2},{x:60,sz:1},{x:120,sz:1.5},
        {x:90,sz:2.5},{x:150,sz:1},{x:40,sz:1.5},{x:100,sz:1},{x:130,sz:2},
      ];
      particles.forEach((p, i) => {
        const delay = (0.05 + i * 0.06).toFixed(2);
        const finalY = 152 - p.sz;
        const op = (0.35 - i * 0.01).toFixed(2);
        bf += `<circle cx="${p.x}" cy="10" r="${p.sz}" fill="#A89070" opacity="0">
          <animate attributeName="opacity" from="0" to="${Math.max(0.1, parseFloat(op))}" dur="0.2s" fill="freeze" begin="${delay}s"/>
          <animate attributeName="cy" from="10" to="${finalY}" dur="0.5s" fill="freeze" begin="${delay}s" calcMode="spline" keySplines="0.55 0.085 0.68 0.53"/>
        </circle>`;
      });
      bf += `<text x="100" y="180" text-anchor="middle" font-family="JetBrains Mono" font-size="8" fill="#8B7355" opacity="0">
        TERRAIN<animate attributeName="opacity" from="0" to="0.4" dur="0.4s" fill="freeze" begin="1s"/>
      </text>`;
      return bf;

    default: return '';
  }
}

function initEntropyViz() {
  buildEntropyChart();
  buildEntropyPhaseButtons();
  setEntropyPhase(0);
}

// MOSAIC
const mosaicColors = ['#C4533A','#F0ECE4','#8B7355','#5B7A6B','#C4A95A','#3A3530','#E8785F','#A89070'];
function renderMosaic() {
  const grid = document.getElementById('mosaicGrid');
  grid.innerHTML = '';
  for (let i = 0; i < 72; i++) {
    const tile = document.createElement('div');
    tile.className = 'mosaic-tile';
    tile.style.background = mosaicColors[Math.floor(Math.random() * mosaicColors.length)];
    grid.appendChild(tile);
  }
}

function shuffleMosaic() {
  const tiles = document.querySelectorAll('.mosaic-tile');
  tiles.forEach(tile => {
    tile.style.transition = 'all 0.5s';
    tile.style.transform = 'scale(0)';
    setTimeout(() => {
      tile.style.background = mosaicColors[Math.floor(Math.random() * mosaicColors.length)];
      tile.style.transform = 'scale(1)';
    }, 200 + Math.random() * 400);
  });
}

// TIMELINE
function renderTimeline() {
  const wrap = document.getElementById('timelineWrap');
  timelineData.forEach(t => {
    wrap.innerHTML += `
      <div class="timeline-item">
        <div class="timeline-dot"></div>
        <div class="timeline-date">${t.date}</div>
        <div class="timeline-title">${t.title}</div>
        <div class="timeline-text">${t.text}</div>
      </div>
    `;
  });
}

// NAV
let laborAnimated = false;
document.querySelectorAll('.nav-bar button').forEach(btn => {
  btn.addEventListener('click', () => {
    document.querySelectorAll('.nav-bar button').forEach(b => b.classList.remove('active'));
    btn.classList.add('active');
    
    document.querySelectorAll('.section').forEach(s => s.classList.remove('active'));
    const section = document.getElementById('sec-' + btn.dataset.section);
    if (section) section.classList.add('active');
    
    if (btn.dataset.section === 'labor' && !laborAnimated) {
      laborAnimated = true;
      setTimeout(animateLabor, 200);
    }
  });
});

// INIT
renderMap();
renderBeyondRome();
renderFlows();
renderMaterials();
renderMosaic();
renderTimeline();
initEntropyViz();
</script>
</body>
</html>
